qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Parameterized Content-Type bypass restores blocked HTML upload on extensionless files
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding targets file-upload extension validation in FilesRouter and FilesController. It does not involve the advisory's social-login authentication adapters, missing clientId/appIds enforcement, or JWT audience-claim validation.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's root cause: Parse Server accepting Google, Apple, or Facebook identity tokens without enforcing JWT audience validation through required clientId/appIds configuration in the auth adapters.
evidence: The advisory describes an authentication bypass in src/Adapters/Auth/google.js, src/Adapters/Auth/apple.js, and src/Adapters/Auth/facebook.js where JWT verification can skip or omit audience validation.
The file_upload_html_bypass finding is limited to HTML file-upload filtering and MIME-based filename extension resolution in FilesRouter and FilesController, not social-login token verification.
